cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out this problem. So the question is to compute 3 times (1 plus 3 times (1 plus 3 times (1 plus 3 times (1 plus 3)))). Hmm, that looks a bit complicated with all the parentheses, but I think if I take it step by step, starting from the innermost part, it should work out. First, let me write down the expression as given: 3(1 + 3(1 + 3(1 + 3(1 + 3)))) Starting from the innermost bracket, which is (1 + 3). Let me compute that first. 1 plus 3 is 4. So replacing that back into the expression, the next layer out would be: 3(1 + 3(1 + 3(1 + 3(4)))) Now, moving one level up, the next part to compute is the 3(4). Let me do that. 3 times 4 is 12. So now the expression becomes: 3(1 + 3(1 + 3(1 + 12))) Now, adding 1 and 12
